events.title: Olaparib Approved for BRCA-Mutated Breast Cancer
events.detail: On January 12, 2018, the FDA approved olaparib (Lynparza) for adults with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline BRCA-mutated, HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer who have been treated with prior chemotherapy. This was the first PARP inhibitor approved for breast cancer, validated by the OlympiAD trial showing a 42% reduction in disease progression vs. standard chemotherapy.

sources.name: New England Journal of Medicine — OlympiAD trial

events.title: Durvalumab Approved for Unresectable Stage III NSCLC
events.detail: On February 16, 2018, the FDA approved durvalumab (Imfinzi) for unresectable, stage III non-small cell lung cancer in patients whose disease has not progressed following concurrent platinum-based chemoradiation therapy. The landmark PACIFIC trial showed durvalumab maintenance extended median progression-free survival from 5.6 to 16.8 months, establishing immunotherapy consolidation as standard of care.

events.title: Kymriah Expanded to Adult Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma
events.detail: On May 1, 2018, the FDA approved tisagenlecleucel (Kymriah) for adult patients with relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma after two or more lines of systemic therapy. This expansion, supported by the JULIET trial showing a 54% overall response rate including 40% complete responses, marked CAR-T cell therapy entering the mainstream adult oncology market.

sources.name: The Lancet — JULIET trial (Schuster et al.)

events.title: Pembrolizumab Approved for Relapsed/Refractory Cervical Cancer
events.detail: On June 12, 2018, the FDA granted accelerated approval to pembrolizumab (Keytruda) for patients with recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer with disease progression on or after chemotherapy whose tumors express PD-L1. The KEYNOTE-158 trial showed a 14.3% overall response rate with a median duration of response not reached at time of analysis, signaling immunotherapy's reach into gynecologic cancers.

events.title: Nobel Prize in Medicine for Immune Checkpoint Therapy
events.detail: On October 1, 2018, the Nobel Assembly at Karolinska Institutet awarded the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine jointly to James P. Allison (MD Anderson Cancer Center) and Tasuku Honjo (Kyoto University) for their discovery of cancer therapy by inhibition of negative immune regulation. Allison's work on CTLA-4 and Honjo's discovery of PD-1 as immune checkpoints laid the scientific foundation for drugs like ipilimumab and pembrolizumab, which have transformed survival in multiple cancers.

events.title: CheckMate-067 5-Year Data: Ipilimumab+Nivolumab Doubles Melanoma Survival
events.detail: At ESMO 2018, five-year follow-up data from CheckMate-067 showed that the combination of nivolumab plus ipilimumab achieved a 52% five-year overall survival rate in advanced melanoma, compared to 44% for nivolumab alone and 26% for ipilimumab alone. This landmark result demonstrated durable, possibly curative responses for a disease where median survival had previously been less than one year.

events.title: KEYNOTE-522 Transforms Triple-Negative Breast Cancer Treatment
events.detail: Results from KEYNOTE-522 presented at ESMO 2019 showed that adding pembrolizumab to neoadjuvant chemotherapy significantly increased pathologic complete response rates in early-stage, high-risk triple-negative breast cancer (64.8% vs. 51.2%). This trial would become the basis for the 2021 FDA approval and fundamentally altered neoadjuvant treatment paradigms for TNBC.

events.title: COVID-19 Pandemic Disrupts Global Cancer Trials — But Accelerates mRNA Technology
events.detail: The COVID-19 pandemic beginning in March 2020 caused widespread disruption to cancer clinical trials, with over half of active US oncology trials temporarily paused or modified. However, the pandemic-driven acceleration of mRNA vaccine technology at Moderna and BioNTech created critical infrastructure and regulatory familiarity that would later accelerate mRNA cancer vaccine development. Patient enrollment delays were estimated to have caused months-long setbacks to drug development timelines.

sources.name: JAMA Oncology — COVID impact on cancer trials

events.title: FDA Approves First TMB-H Tumor-Agnostic Indication for Pembrolizumab
events.detail: On June 16, 2020, the FDA granted accelerated approval to pembrolizumab for adult and pediatric patients with unresectable or metastatic tumor mutational burden-high (TMB-H, ≥10 mut/Mb) solid tumors that have progressed after prior treatment. This was the first approval based solely on a genomic biomarker (TMB) rather than tumor histology — a landmark in biomarker-driven, tissue-agnostic oncology.

events.title: Tecartus Approved — First CAR-T for Mantle Cell Lymphoma
events.detail: On July 24, 2020, the FDA approved brexucabtagene autoleucel (Tecartus, Kite/Gilead) for adults with relapsed or refractory mantle cell lymphoma. The ZUMA-2 trial showed an 87% overall response rate with 62% complete responses in this aggressive B-cell lymphoma. This was the first CAR-T therapy approval for MCL and demonstrated expanding applicability of adoptive cell therapies.

events.title: Pembrolizumab Approved for First-Line Metastatic Triple-Negative Breast Cancer
events.detail: On November 13, 2020, the FDA granted accelerated approval to pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy for locally recurrent unresectable or metastatic triple-negative breast cancer in adults whose tumors express PD-L1 (CPS ≥10). Based on KEYNOTE-355, this approval brought immunotherapy into first-line treatment of the most aggressive form of breast cancer, which disproportionately affects younger women.

events.title: FDA Clears AI Mammography Tools — New Era for Radiologic Screening
events.detail: By late 2020, the FDA had cleared several AI-powered mammography reading tools, including ProFound AI (iCAD) which demonstrated ability to detect breast cancer 52% earlier than standard reading. Studies published in The Lancet Digital Health and Nature showed AI systems matching or exceeding radiologist performance on screening mammography datasets, spurring rapid clinical adoption. The AI diagnostic tool landscape in oncology began expanding rapidly.

events.title: Nivolumab + Ipilimumab Approved First-Line in NSCLC
events.detail: On May 15, 2020, the FDA approved nivolumab (Opdivo) plus ipilimumab (Yervoy) as first-line treatment for metastatic or recurrent NSCLC with no EGFR/ALK alterations and PD-L1 ≥1%. The CheckMate-227 trial showed improved overall survival vs. chemotherapy. This approval established the first dual checkpoint blockade regimen as standard first-line care in lung cancer.

events.title: Breyanzi Approved — CD19 CAR-T for Large B-Cell Lymphoma
events.detail: On February 5, 2021, the FDA approved lisocabtagene maraleucel (Breyanzi, Bristol Myers Squibb) for adults with relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma after two or more lines of systemic therapy. The TRANSCEND NHL 001 trial showed a 73% overall response rate with 53% complete responses. Breyanzi's 4-1BB costimulatory domain design was associated with lower rates of severe cytokine release syndrome compared to earlier CAR-T products.

sources.name: The Lancet — TRANSCEND NHL 001 (Abramson et al.)

events.title: Abecma Approved — First Anti-BCMA CAR-T for Multiple Myeloma
events.detail: On March 26, 2021, the FDA approved idecabtagene vicleucel (Abecma, BMS/bluebird bio) for adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma after four or more prior lines of therapy. This was the first CAR-T cell therapy approved for multiple myeloma and the first targeting B-cell maturation antigen (BCMA). The KarMMa trial showed an 88% overall response rate including 39% complete responses, representing a major advance in a historically incurable disease.

events.title: Pembrolizumab Approved for High-Risk Early TNBC — Curative Intent
events.detail: On July 26, 2021, the FDA approved pembrolizumab for neoadjuvant then adjuvant treatment of high-risk early-stage triple-negative breast cancer (stage II-III). KEYNOTE-522 showed a significant improvement in event-free survival (84.5% vs. 76.8% at 3 years) in addition to higher pathologic complete response rates. This marked immunotherapy entering the curative-intent treatment of early breast cancer for the first time.

sources.name: NEJM — KEYNOTE-522 updated results (Schmid et al.)

events.title: Cemiplimab (Libtayo) Approved for Recurrent Cervical Cancer
events.detail: On September 23, 2021, the FDA approved cemiplimab-rwlc (Libtayo, Regeneron/Sanofi) for recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer with disease progression on or after platinum chemotherapy. EMPOWER-Cervical 1 showed a significant overall survival improvement (12.0 vs. 8.5 months) with a 31% reduction in mortality risk. This was the first second-line systemic therapy to demonstrate an overall survival benefit in cervical cancer.

events.title: CARTITUDE-1 Pivotal Data: Carvykti Achieves 98% Response in Myeloma
events.detail: At ASH 2021, pivotal data from the CARTITUDE-1 trial showed ciltacabtagene autoleucel (Carvykti) achieving an unprecedented 97.9% overall response rate in heavily pre-treated relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, with 78.4% of patients achieving stringent complete response. Median progression-free survival had not been reached at 18 months. These results supported the subsequent FDA approval and positioned Carvykti as potentially the most active CAR-T product in myeloma.

events.title: First CRISPR-Edited T-Cell Cancer Trial Reports Early Safety Data
events.detail: In February 2021, researchers at Penn Medicine published the first in-human safety results of CRISPR-Cas9-edited T-cell therapy for cancer in Nature Medicine. Three patients with advanced refractory cancers received T-cells with three genes disrupted by CRISPR editing. The study demonstrated feasibility and acceptable safety, with modified cells persisting for up to 9 months, establishing a proof-of-concept for next-generation gene-edited cell therapies.

events.title: Carvykti Approved — Second Anti-BCMA CAR-T Sets New Efficacy Bar
events.detail: On February 28, 2022, the FDA approved ciltacabtagene autoleucel (Carvykti, J&J/Legend Biotech) for adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma after four or more prior lines of therapy including a PI, IMiD, and anti-CD38 antibody. Supported by CARTITUDE-1 showing 97.9% overall response, Carvykti achieved the highest response rate of any CAR-T therapy and positioned as a potential game-changer for late-line myeloma treatment.

events.title: DESTINY-Breast04: Trastuzumab Deruxtecan Redefines HER2-Low Breast Cancer
events.detail: At ASCO 2022 and published in NEJM, DESTINY-Breast04 showed that trastuzumab deruxtecan (Enhertu, AstraZeneca/Daiichi Sankyo) significantly improved progression-free survival (9.9 vs. 5.1 months) and overall survival (23.4 vs. 16.8 months) in HER2-low metastatic breast cancer vs. physician's choice chemotherapy. The FDA approved this indication on August 5, 2022. By redefining "HER2-low" as a targetable patient population, this approval extended ADC eligibility to approximately 60% of metastatic breast cancer patients previously ineligible for HER2-directed therapy.

events.title: Mirvetuximab Soravtansine Approved — First ADC for Ovarian Cancer
events.detail: On November 14, 2022, the FDA granted accelerated approval to mirvetuximab soravtansine-gynx (ELAHERE, ImmunoGen) for folate receptor alpha (FRα)-positive, platinum-resistant epithelial ovarian cancer in adults who received 1–3 prior systemic treatment regimens. The SORAYA trial showed a 32.4% objective response rate with a median response duration of 6.9 months. This was the first FRα-targeting ADC and the first new mechanism approved in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer in years.

sources.name: Journal of Clinical Oncology — SORAYA trial

events.title: Teclistamab — First Bispecific BCMA × CD3 Antibody Approved for Myeloma
events.detail: On October 25, 2022, the FDA granted accelerated approval to teclistamab-cqyv (Tecvayli, J&J) for relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma after 4 or more prior lines of therapy. MajesTEC-1 showed a 63% overall response rate in heavily pre-treated patients with an off-the-shelf, ready-to-use product vs. personalized CAR-T manufacturing. Bispecific antibodies emerged as a scalable, accessible alternative to CAR-T cell therapy for myeloma.

events.title: mRNA-4157 Phase 2b Data: 44% Reduction in Melanoma Recurrence
events.detail: At AACR Annual Meeting in April 2023, Moderna and MSD (Merck) announced Phase 2b results from KEYNOTE-942 showing that the personalized mRNA cancer vaccine mRNA-4157/V940 plus pembrolizumab reduced the risk of recurrence or death by 44% versus pembrolizumab alone in patients with high-risk, stage III/IV resected melanoma. This was the first randomized trial demonstrating clinical benefit for a personalized neoantigen mRNA cancer vaccine, representing a landmark in the field. The New England Journal of Medicine published detailed data in 2023.

sources.name: AACR Annual Meeting 2023 — Moderna/MSD press release

events.title: mRNA-4157 Phase 3 Trial (KEYNOTE-942P3) Initiated in Melanoma
events.detail: Following the landmark Phase 2b results, Moderna and MSD initiated a Phase 3 randomized controlled trial of mRNA-4157/V940 plus pembrolizumab versus pembrolizumab alone in 2023. The trial targets patients with resected high-risk stage II-IV melanoma. Personalized neoantigen vaccines require ~6 weeks to manufacture based on tumor whole-exome sequencing, positioning this as a transformative but complex precision oncology approach. FDA granted breakthrough therapy designation to the combination.

events.title: Elranatamab Approved — Second BCMA×CD3 Bispecific Expands Options in Myeloma
events.detail: On August 14, 2023, the FDA granted accelerated approval to elranatamab-bcmm (Elrexfio, Pfizer) for relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma after four or more prior lines of therapy. MagnetisMM-3 showed 61% overall response rate. The availability of multiple bispecific antibodies in myeloma — alongside CAR-T options — created a rich competitive therapeutic landscape offering patients various risk-benefit tradeoffs between off-the-shelf and personalized cellular approaches.

events.title: Multi-Cancer Liquid Biopsy Tests Enter Clinical Workflow
events.detail: By 2023, multi-cancer early detection (MCED) blood tests using circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) methylation analysis had advanced substantially in clinical validation. Grail's Galleri test, designed to detect 50+ cancer types from a single blood draw, received commercial availability and began real-world evidence generation. Studies in NEJM showed the test could detect cancers of types that currently have no standard screening — such as pancreatic, ovarian, and head-and-neck cancers — at earlier, more treatable stages.

era: TIL Therapy, mRNA Vaccines & AI Frontiers (2024–2026)

events.title: Amtagvi (Lifileucel) Approved — World's First TIL Cell Therapy
events.detail: On February 16, 2024, the FDA approved lifileucel (Amtagvi, Iovance Biotherapeutics) for adults with unresectable or metastatic melanoma previously treated with a PD-1 blocking antibody and, if BRAF V600-mutant, a BRAF inhibitor ± MEK inhibitor. C-144-01 showed a 31.5% objective response rate including durable complete responses. This was the first-ever approval of a tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) therapy and the first cell therapy not engineered with an exogenous receptor, marking a new treatment modality for solid tumors.

sources.name: Nature Medicine — C-144-01 trial (Chesney et al.)

events.title: Aucatzyl Approved — CAR-T with Faster Manufacturing for Adult ALL
events.detail: On November 8, 2024, the FDA approved obecabtagene autoleucel (Aucatzyl, Autolus Therapeutics) for adults with relapsed or refractory B-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia. The FELIX trial showed a 70% complete remission rate in a disease where adult outcomes remain poor. Notably, Aucatzyl uses a novel CD19-targeting CAR construct with potentially reduced tonic signaling and prolonged T-cell persistence, representing next-generation CAR-T design innovation.

events.title: mRNA-4157 Trials Expand to NSCLC and Other Solid Tumors
events.detail: In 2024, Moderna and MSD expanded the mRNA-4157/V940 (combined with pembrolizumab) clinical program to include non-small cell lung cancer, bladder cancer, and renal cell carcinoma following the melanoma Phase 2b success. Phase 2 trials across multiple tumor types are ongoing, exploring whether personalized neoantigen vaccines with personalized tumor mutational profiles will show similar benefit in other high-TMB cancers. The program represents the largest personalized cancer vaccine initiative in clinical history.

sources.name: STAT News — Moderna oncology pipeline reporting

events.title: AI Cancer Detection Achieves Clinical-Grade Performance Across Multiple Cancer Types
events.detail: By 2024, AI diagnostic systems demonstrated clinical-grade performance across multiple cancer screening modalities. Google DeepMind's AI for lung cancer CT screening, Sybil for lung cancer risk prediction from CT, and multiple FDA-cleared AI mammography tools achieved radiologist-equivalent or superior sensitivity in prospective clinical settings. The FDA's AI/ML-based Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) action plan saw over 100+ AI oncology diagnostic tools cleared by 2024, signaling a structural shift in how cancer is detected.

sources.name: Nature Medicine — AI cancer detection reviews

events.title: CRISPR-Edited CAR-T Therapies Enter Phase 2 Trials for Solid Tumors
events.detail: By 2025, multiple CRISPR-edited allogeneic ('off-the-shelf') CAR-T therapies advanced into Phase 2 clinical trials, including programs from Intellia Therapeutics, Caribou Biosciences, and Allogene. Unlike autologous CAR-T requiring weeks of personalized manufacturing, allogeneic CAR-T can be manufactured at scale and administered within days. Early Phase 1 data showed CRISPR-disrupted T-cell rejection genes could extend persistence of donor T-cells in solid tumor settings — a major step toward making cell therapy accessible to the ~60% of patients who lack time or biology for standard CAR-T manufacturing.

events.title: ADC + Immunotherapy Combinations Win Approvals — New Standard in Multiple Cancers
events.detail: By 2025, combinations of antibody-drug conjugates with immune checkpoint inhibitors had received multiple FDA approvals, including trastuzumab deruxtecan plus pembrolizumab in HER2-positive breast cancer and sacituzumab govitecan combinations in TNBC. These combinations leveraged the immunogenic cell death induced by ADC payloads to potentiate checkpoint blockade responses, achieving outcomes neither modality achieved alone. The paradigm of "chemo-immunotherapy ADC combinations" became the new standard in several aggressive breast and urothelial cancer subtypes.
